title: Python
description: Versatile programming. Data structures, OOP, modules, file I/O, web frameworks, automation and scripting.

items.label: Variables & Data Types
items.code:
name = "John"         # str
age = 25               # int
height = 5.9           # float
is_student = True      # bool
hobbies = ["read", "code"]  # list
person = {"name": "John", "age": 25}  # dict
coordinates = (10, 20)  # tuple
unique = {1, 2, 3}      # set

print(f"{name} is {age} years old")
print(type(variable))     # check type

# Dynamic typing
x = 5       # int
x = "hello" # now str - allowed

items.label: Operators
items.code:
# Arithmetic
+ - * /          # basic
//                # floor division (5//2 = 2)
%                 # modulus (5%2 = 1)
**                # exponent (2**3 = 8)

# Comparison
== != < > <= >=

# Logical
and or not

# Identity
is is not

# Membership
in not in

# Walrus operator (3.8+)
if (n := len(x)) > 5:
    print(f"{n} items")

items.label: Conditionals
items.code:
if x > 0:
    print("positive")
elif x == 0:
    print("zero")
else:
    print("negative")

# Ternary
status = "adult" if age >= 18 else "minor"

# Match-case (3.10+)
match status:
    case 200:
        print("OK")
    case 404:
        print("Not found")
    case _:
        print("Unknown")

items.label: Loops
items.code:
# For loop
for i in range(5):      # 0,1,2,3,4
    print(i)

for i, item in enumerate(items):
    print(i, item)

for key, val in person.items():
    print(key, val)

# While loop
while count > 0:
    count -= 1
    if count == 3:
        break       # exit loop
    if count == 5:
        continue    # skip to next iteration

# List comprehension
squares = [x**2 for x in range(10)]
evens = [x for x in range(10) if x % 2 == 0]

items.label: Function Definitions
List Operations
items.code:
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits.append("date")       # add to end
fruits.insert(0, "apricot") # add at index
fruits.remove("banana")     # remove by value
popped = fruits.pop()       # remove and return last
idx = fruits.index("apple") # find index
"apple" in fruits            # membership check
fruits.sort()               # sort in-place
sorted(fruits)              # return new sorted
fruits.reverse()
[f.upper() for f in fruits]  # comprehension

items.label: Dict & Set Operations
Classes & Inheritance
File Operations
items.code:
# Read
with open("file.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
    lines = f.readlines()
    for line in f:
        print(line)

# Write
with open("file.txt", "w") as f:    # overwrite
    f.write("Hello")

with open("file.txt", "a") as f:    # append
    f.write("More text\n")

# Context managers
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def open_file(name):
    f = open(name, 'w')
    try:
        yield f
    finally:
        f.close()

items.label: Modules & Imports
items.code:
# Importing
import os
import sys
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from math import sqrt as square_root

# Your module (my_module.py)
def my_func():
    pass

# Import it
from my_module import my_func

# __name__ == "__main__" guard
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Run only when executed directly
    main()

items.label: Try/Except/Finally
Standard Library
items.code:
import os, sys, json, re, math, random, datetime

os.getcwd()          # current dir
os.listdir(".")      # list files
os.path.exists(path)  # check exists

json.dumps(data)      # dict -> JSON string
json.loads(json_str)  # JSON string -> dict

re.search(pattern, text)
re.findall(r"\d+", text)
re.sub(old, new, text)

random.randint(1, 10)
random.choice(items)
random.shuffle(list)

math.sqrt(16), math.pi, math.floor(3.7)

datetime.now()
datetime.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

from collections import Counter, defaultdict, deque
from itertools import chain, groupby, product
from functools import reduce, lru_cache

items.label: Third-party Libraries
items.code:
# pip install requests
import requests
r = requests.get('https://api.github.com')
r.status_code  # 200
r.json()       # parsed JSON

# pip install flask
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/api/hello')
def hello():
    return jsonify({"message": "Hello"})

# pip install pandas
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
df.head()
df.describe()
df.groupby('column').mean()

# pip install pytest
def test_example():
    assert add(2, 3) == 5
